parsing_approach: Comment- and string-aware masking pass ('--', '/* */', single-quoted strings incl. '' escapes), then statement splitting on top-level semicolons and per-kind extraction. Semicolons in strings/comments never break statements; commented-out DDL is never extracted.
extracts: one SqlStatement node per statement with kind, target object, line span
CREATE TABLE columns and REFERENCES targets (inline and table-level FK)
foreign-key 'references' edges between tables defined in the same script
known_limitations: Not a SQL grammar: column types/constraints are not modeled, only column names.
Dialect-specific statements outside the known-kind list are kept as kind UNKNOWN.
Double-quoted identifiers are unquoted naively (no embedded-quote handling).
Trigger bodies with BEGIN...END containing semicolons will be split (document per-repo if triggers are present).
